element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Available from start; player learns basic firing patterns
Second weapon type; player learns to switch between weapon types
Third weapon type; player learns tactical switching
Top-down perspective with pseudo-3D warping effects; teaches spatial awareness
Side-scrolling perspective; requires different weapon selection strategy
Mandatory gauntlet; one at end of each stage; tests weapon selection and dodging
Player gains new weapon option after each stage completion; must plan loadout for next stage
Specialized weapon for specific stage threats
Area-denial weapon; effective against clustered enemies
Game difficulty escalates; enemy fire becomes more complex

typical_tension_triggers: Stage transition (layout switch from vertical to horizontal or vice versa)
Weapon disabled from hit (reduced to weak default)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating stage format (vertical/horizontal) creates rhythm shifts; tension builds through enemy density and bullet patterns toward boss fights; relief granted by stage completion and weapon selection downtime

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate; player learns three weapon types and vertical/horizontal switching mechanics through Stages 1-2 (Cumuluses, Tralieb)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating complexity; Stages 3-4 (Urbanite, Cavern) introduce more specialized weapons and denser enemy patterns
difficulty_curve.late_game: High intensity; Stages 5-6 (Lava Planet, Armada Fortress) feature advanced bullet patterns and boss challenges
notable_spikes: First boss encounter (Stage 1 end) - introduces mandatory boss mechanic
Stage 6 final fortress - culmination of all mechanics
Loop 2+ difficulty increases - denser bullet patterns on replay

notable_level_patterns.description: Stages alternate between top-down vertical scrolling and side-scrolling horizontal layouts, forcing player to select appropriate weapons for each perspective and enemy distribution
Each stage culminates in mandatory boss fight; progression locked until boss defeated; boss fight is the tension peak of each level
After each stage clear, player receives new weapon option and modification opportunity; creates strategic planning phase between combat-intense stages
Each of 6 stages represents different location (cloud planet, space colony, urban world, water cavern, lava planet, enemy fortress); visual/thematic variety maintains engagement
